text: In release-please.yml the build job uses 'if: needs.release-please.outputs.release_created == "true"' rather than the shorter truthy form shown in the release-please README. The README example puts the if on steps within the same job, where unset outputs are empty strings (falsy). When the value is passed through a job-level output to another job, release-please emits the literal string 'false' on non-release pushes, which GitHub Actions evaluates as truthy (any non-empty string is truthy). Without the explicit '== true' comparison the build job would run on every push to main and fail trying to upload to an empty tag. Do not simplify this back.
